name: Dunsany's Chess
author: AGP
description: Lord Dunsany's asymmetric variant (1942). Black has the standard 16-piece chess army; White has no king and instead fields 32 pawns filling ranks 1-4. Black moves first. Black wins by capturing all 32 white pawns; White wins by checkmating Black's king. Only Black's pawns get the initial two-square move; White's pawns never double-step. White has no king, so it is never in check.
